entities.name: ADOPTION OF APPROPRIATE TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: The process of smallholder farmers implementing suitable agricultural technologies to mitigate climate effects

entities.name: SMALL-SCALE FARMING
entities.description: Agricultural production practiced by smallholder farmers

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE AND VARIABILITY
entities.description: Environmental shifts and fluctuations affecting agricultural stability

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: The continent where climate change effects on small-scale farming are observed

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The country where the study on technology adoption was conducted

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: A climatic variable used to match study sites

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: A climatic variable that differs between study sites and influences technology use

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Regions in Kenya characterized by low rainfall

entities.name: SUB-HUMID REGIONS
entities.description: Regions in Kenya with higher humidity and rainfall than semi-arid regions

entities.name: COOL REGIONS
entities.description: Regions characterized by lower temperatures

entities.name: WARM REGIONS
entities.description: Regions characterized by higher temperatures

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY METHODS
entities.description: Research approach involving focus-group discussions and household sampling

entities.name: FOCUS-GROUP DISCUSSIONS
entities.description: A qualitative data collection method used in the study

entities.name: RANDOM SAMPLING
entities.description: The process of selecting 722 households for data collection

entities.name: GENDER
entities.description: A demographic factor analyzed for its influence on technology awareness and adoption

entities.name: MALE-HEADED HOUSEHOLDS
entities.description: Households led by males, showing higher adoption rates

entities.name: FEMALE-HEADED HOUSEHOLDS
entities.description: Households led by females, showing different adoption rates compared to male-headed ones

entities.name: FARMER-TO-FARMER LEARNING
entities.description: A prominent source of information for technology knowledge and use

entities.name: WATER HARVESTING
entities.description: An appropriate technology used more frequently in warm regions

entities.name: REDUCED TILLAGE
entities.description: An appropriate technology used more frequently in warm regions

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: An appropriate technology used more frequently in warm regions

entities.name: GREEN MANURE
entities.description: An appropriate technology used more frequently in warm regions

entities.name: MULCHES
entities.description: An appropriate technology used more frequently in warm regions
